script: s241_gatecal.py (legacy filename; is the plan's Phase 0 implementation)
joint_verdict: BOTH MODELS HALT — GATE-CAL not met on GPT-J OR Qwen; metric not calibrated with the current stimulus sets. Do NOT promote to Phase 1.

qwen.finding: INVERTED / NO DYNAMIC RANGE: counterfact (0.019) and destructive (0.024) are BELOW the null floor (0.038). Even a max-magnitude (destructive) edit barely perturbs same-entity probes. The Qwen facts edit a FICTIONAL/peripheral attribute ('signature instrument associated with X') disconnected from the probed real attributes -> the plan's 'Flex Tape'/attribute-selection artifact. The null edit re-asserts degenerate top-1 tokens (' ____', ' He') which perturb MORE than a clean counterfact target, inverting floor vs counterfact.

reproduction.prior_run_json: s241_gatecal_gptj.prior_0127.json (recovered; was lost to a disconnect)
reproduction.status: REPRODUCED — current run independently reproduces the prior HALT

findings: GPT-J same-entity JS metric has NO clean ~0 floor: a NULL/identity MEMIT edit (re-assert the model's current top-1) still drifts mean ~0.11 JS on other same-entity attributes; Curie (degenerate null target ' the') dominates at 0.245.

kl_factor (essence-KL regularizer) is NEARLY INERT for same-entity drift: ~0.01 JS movement across a 16x sweep (0.0->1.0) and not cleanly monotone. The §8.2 KL safeguard is cross-entity, not same-entity (corroborates plan thesis).
Destructive ceiling (0.394) ~= counterfact (0.386): same-entity drift is already near-ceiling at normal edit strength; thin operational->ceiling band.
Cross-entity JS ~= 0 throughout (the MEMIT-lineage Neighborhood-Success control stays clean).

interpretation_fork.A_harness_or_metric_artifact: A truly null edit should not drift; ~0.11 floor may reflect MEMIT re-solve perturbation even at zero target delta, or fp16 inference noise in low-prob tails picked up by full-distribution JS. Would warrant metric/harness refinement before Phase 1.
interpretation_fork.B_real_signal: MEMIT cannot even re-assert an existing value without collateral same-entity damage -> strongest evidence for same-entity non-locality; the 'floor' is the intrinsic MEMIT same-entity noise and should be adopted as the per-model normalization baseline (read drift relative to it) rather than treated as harness failure.

gptj.interpretation_note: Earlier fork resolved: Qwen now run. GPT-J floor-high and Qwen floor-inverted are BOTH stimulus artifacts (attribute centrality), not a clean model-locality difference.

joint_conclusion.summary: GATE-CAL FAILS on both models, in OPPOSITE ways, for the SAME root cause: the fact/probe stimulus sets are not centrality-matched. GPT-J edits CENTRAL well-encoded attributes (Eiffel location, Jordan sport) -> high same-entity bleed even at the null edit -> floor too high (0.11). Qwen edits a FICTIONAL peripheral attribute ('signature instrument') -> near-total isolation -> counterfact(0.019)/destructive(0.024) BELOW the null floor(0.038) -> no dynamic range, inverted. Neither yields floor~=0 with an ordered floor<counterfact<ceiling band.
joint_conclusion.implication: The GPT-J-vs-Qwen drift gap is largely a STIMULUS (attribute-centrality) artifact, NOT yet evidence of a model-level same-entity-locality difference. The JS-on-same-entity-probes metric is sound; the STIMULUS design is the defect and is the Phase-0 precondition that must be fixed before Phase 1.
joint_conclusion.kl_factor: On BOTH models kl_factor is ~inert for same-entity drift (GPT-J ~0.01 over 16x; Qwen 0.050->0.033) -> the essence-KL §8.2 safeguard is cross-entity, not same-entity (corroborates plan).

recommended_next: REDESIGN the GATE-CAL stimulus: pick facts where (a) the edit prompt has a CONFIDENT REAL top-1 so the NULL/identity edit re-asserts a real token (drives the floor toward 0 and avoids degenerate ' ____'/' the'/' He' targets), and (b) the counterfact edits a REAL central attribute and the probe battery is OTHER real central attributes the model confidently encodes. Match attribute centrality between edit and probes across both models.
Re-run GATE-CAL with the redesigned set; PASS requires floor~=0 AND floor<counterfact<=destructive on BOTH models, with the metric responsive.
Only then promote to Phase 1. Per CLAUDE.md halt discipline: HALT here; do NOT run Phase 1 on an uncalibrated metric.
